data.problems:
• Problem 1: Environmental Risk Alert Application
• Problem 2: Personal Medical History Vault
• Problem 3: Smart Campus Services Hub
• Problem 4: Document-Based Adaptive Quiz and Flashcard System

intents.name: problem_1_details
patterns: What is the first problem statement?
What is the environmental risk app?
What do we build for problem 1?
Can you explain the first project?

data.title: Problem 1: Environmental Risk Alert Application
data.objective: Build an application that monitors environmental conditions for a user's location and alerts the user only when conditions become harmful or dangerous.
data.requirements:
• Obtain environmental data (such as AQI, weather, UV index, temperature, or traffic) for a specific location.
• Evaluate the incoming data to detect dangerous conditions.
• Notify the user only in severe cases, deliberately avoiding unnecessary or spam alerts.
• Clearly display the reason for each generated alert.
data.outcome: A fully functional working system that alerts users only during genuinely high-risk environmental conditions.

intents.name: problem_2_details
patterns: What is the second problem statement?
What is the medical history vault?
What do we build for problem 2?
Details for personal medical history vault
Can you explain the second project?

data.title: Problem 2: Personal Medical History Vault
data.objective: Build a secure application to organize all personal medical records, photos of medicines, prescriptions, and scan results in one centralized place with smart categorization.
data.requirements:
• Allow users to upload photos and images of medicines, prescriptions, dental records, and medical scans.
• Implement image recognition or manual tagging to auto-categorize files by type (e.g., Dental, General Health, Prescription, Lab Results, Vaccinations).
• Store clinical visit history including dates, doctor details, and notes, enabling a search function by date or condition.
• Provide system reminders only for expiring prescriptions or upcoming checkups.
• Enable the exporting of organized records as a PDF document for seamless sharing with doctors.
data.outcome: A complete, secure personal health archive that is easy to navigate and instantly accessible during medical emergencies.

intents.name: problem_3_details
patterns: What is the third problem statement?
What is the smart campus app?
What do we build for problem 3?
Details for smart campus services hub
Can you explain the third project?

data.title: Problem 3: Smart Campus Services Hub
data.objective: Build a unified application that consolidates all non-academic campus services (such as the canteen, maintenance, lost & found, and transport) into one responsive platform.
data.requirements:
• Canteen Module: Enable pre-ordering with live kitchen status updates and pickup ETAs.
• Maintenance Module: Allow issue reporting utilizing photo uploads and GPS pins for cleanliness, electricity, or plumbing fixes.
• Lost & Found Module: Support image uploads and automatically notify the finder when a potential match is reported.
• Event Discovery: Implement department-wise push notifications for upcoming workshops and fests.
• Campus Map: Integrate an interactive campus map featuring a 'you-are-here' dot and pathfinding logic to any building.
data.outcome: A single application that replaces multiple WhatsApp groups and manual administrative processes, saving students 30+ minutes daily on campus logistics.

intents.name: problem_4_details
patterns: What is the fourth problem statement?
What do we build for problem 4?
Can you explain the fourth project?

data.title: Problem 4: Document-Based Adaptive Quiz and Flashcard System
data.objective: Create a system that allows users to upload academic material (slides, PDFs, assignments, notes) and automatically converts them into interactive quizzes and flashcards utilizing spaced repetition.
data.requirements:
• Data Extraction: Automatically extract questions and key concepts from uploaded files to generate MCQs and flashcards.
• Smart Retry Logic: Ensure incorrect questions reappear to the user at strategically spaced intervals (e.g., 10 min → 1 hr → 1 day).
• Mastery Heatmap: Display visual, topic-wise performance metrics (e.g., Topic A: 92%, Topic B: 34%).
• Practice Modes: Include a 'Timed Quiz Mode' with accuracy tracking and streak indicators, alongside a 'Weak Area Drill' that generates focused questions from low-performing topics.
• Exporting: Allow users to export a report featuring their topic-wise confidence scores and a progress summary.
data.outcome: An intelligent system that seamlessly converts raw academic material into structured learning tools, actively monitoring user performance and adapting to improve the understanding of weaker topics.
